name: HMS Arsenal (FY 140)
events: 30 Dec 1939 At 1355 A/30, south-south-west of the Isle of Man, in position 53°40'N, 05°02'W, HMS Arsenal (Skr C.R. Radford, RNR) reported attacking a doubtful A/S contact with depth charges.
[No German U-boat was in the area though.] (1)
13 Nov 1940 At 0800 hours, HMS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. M.K. Cavenagh-Mainwaring, DSO, RN), made rendes-vous with her escort towards Holy Loch, HMS Arsenal (Skr. C.R. Radford, RNR). (2)
16 Nov 1940 Due to dense fog, the Polish destroyer Burza collided with British ASW trawler HMS Arsenal and suffered bow damage. HMS Arsenal was sunk. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
